title: celtis excelsa
intro: Celtis excelsa, also known as the white stinkwood or weeping celtis, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Africa. The bark and leaves of the Celtis excelsa plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Celtis excelsa include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as the promotion of wound healing and immune system function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Celtis excelsa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional African medicine.
